title: Daily Financial Briefing
marketTone: Double disinflation miss — June CPI and PPI both cool vs consensus, Kospi +6.2% and ASML raised FY26 sales again; Warsh's Senate Day-2 kept the hawkish filter alive while Brent settled $85.90 and China Q2 GDP missed at 4.3%.
summary: June PPI miss (BLS, Jul 15): headline -0.3% m/m, 5.5% y/y (vs unchanged m/m consensus); core ex food/energy +0.2% m/m (vs +0.3%); core ex food/energy/trade +0.1% m/m, 5.1% y/y — second straight disinflation print after Tuesday's CPI.
June CPI downside surprise (BLS, Jul 14): headline -0.4% m/m, 3.5% y/y (vs ~3.8% consensus); core flat m/m, 2.6% y/y (vs ~0.2% m/m and ~2.9% y/y) — largest monthly headline drop since April 2020.

Post-CPI US close (Jul 14): S&P +0.38% to 7,543.59, Nasdaq +0.90% to 26,107.01; 10y Treasury ~4.57% (-~6bp); July Fed hike odds ~17% post-PPI (CME FedWatch/CNBC); COMEX gold settled +1.60% at $4,061.10/oz (Morningstar/Dow Jones).

Asia Wed: Kospi +6.24% to 7,284.41 on SK Hynix +8.83% and CPI relief; Nikkei +1.49% to 68,751; Hang Seng +1.40% to 24,681.10; Shanghai -0.29% to 3,955.58 on Q2 GDP miss and semis profit-taking — Korea/H-shares rally, A-shares lag.
signal: CPI and PPI both missed vs consensus, but Warsh's Senate Day-2 reaffirmed zero tolerance for elevated inflation and Brent settled $85.90 — markets split between 'disinflation confirmed' and 'energy hasn't / Chair won't declare victory.'
watch: TSMC revenue/capex guide (Jul 16) after Kospi +6% and ASML's second FY26 raise — does the AI/memory rebound hold through Taiwan's bellwether?
globalRegime: The Fed holds at 3.50%–3.75%. June CPI and PPI both delivered broad misses vs consensus, pulling July hike odds from ~45% pre-CPI to ~17% post-PPI (CME FedWatch). Chair Warsh's House Q&A (Jul 14) and Senate hearing (Jul 15) reaffirmed zero tolerance for elevated inflation, rejected declaring 'mission accomplished' on one soft print, and offered no forward guidance. Brent settled $85.90 Wednesday (+1.38% from $84.73) as the Iran blockade persists. ASML's second 2026 guidance raise and a Kospi +6% rebound signal the AI/semi trade is alive beneath IBM's -25% warning.
globalChanged: June PPI miss (Jul 15, BLS): headline -0.3% m/m (vs unchanged consensus), 5.5% y/y; core ex food/energy +0.2% m/m (vs +0.3%); core ex food/energy/trade +0.1% m/m, 5.1% y/y; May m/m revised to +0.6% from +1.1% (BLS/CNBC).
June CPI miss (Jul 14, BLS): headline -0.4% m/m (vs -0.2% expected), 3.5% y/y (vs ~3.8%); core flat m/m (vs +0.2%), 2.6% y/y (vs ~2.9%); shelter +0.1% m/m; energy -5.7% m/m.

Post-CPI risk-on US close: S&P +0.38% to 7,543.59, Nasdaq +0.90% to 26,107.01, Dow +0.02% to 52,508.27; 10y yield ~4.57% (-~6bp); July Fed hike probability ~17% vs ~45% Monday; September hike now ~50-50 post-PPI (CME FedWatch; CNBC Jul 15).

Warsh Senate testimony (Jul 15): identical prepared remarks submitted to Senate Banking; House Q&A Jul 14 — 'not mission accomplished' on June CPI, no forward guidance, 'no tolerance' for elevated inflation (Fed/CNN/PBS).

Earnings split: JPM Q2 NI $21.2bn ($7.70 EPS, record quarter); Goldman +9%; IBM -25% to $217.05 on preliminary Q2 miss ($2.93 adj EPS vs $3.01 expected, $17.2bn revenue vs $17.86bn) — worst single-day drop in company history (SEC 8-K/AP/CNBC).

Semis/AI rebound: SK Hynix +8.83% to KRW2,082,000, ADR +27.29% to $193.92; ASML raised FY26 sales to €43–45bn from €36–40bn, Q2 net sales €9.3bn beat (ASML IR Jul 15); Kospi +6.24% to 7,284.41 with buy-side sidecar at open (Yonhap/Businesskorea).

Oil/geopolitics: Brent settled +1.38% at $85.90 Jul 15 (Investing.com/ICE); Jul 14 settle $84.73; US reinstated Iran-only naval blockade, Trump scrapped 20% Hormuz fee for Gulf investment deals (AP/CENTCOM/Bloomberg).
globalImplies: July FOMC hold is now the base case (~83% per CME), but September hike pricing near ~50% post-PPI (CNBC) means back-to-back CPI/PPI misses did not kill the tightening narrative — especially with Brent at $85.90 settle.
June gasoline-driven CPI/PPI relief predates this week's oil spike; July CPI will capture forward energy pressure unless Hormuz tensions ease.

IBM's capex-shift warning (clients buying hardware over software) is a micro read on AI infrastructure crowding out legacy IT spend — bullish for memory/chips, bearish for enterprise software multiples.
globalTensions: CPI/PPI and oil still tell opposite stories — June headline fell on energy that has since surged, and Warsh explicitly warned against declaring victory. Korea/Nasdaq ripped on CPI + ASML while Shanghai slipped -0.29% on GDP miss; gold rebounded on CPI (COMEX $4,061 settle Jul 14) while Brent settled higher Jul 15 — relief, not all-clear.
chinaStance: Moderately loose via quantity, cautious on price — PBOC executed CNY1.4tn 6-month buyout reverse repo Jul 15 (net +CNY500bn after CNY900bn maturity); 7-day rate held at 1.40%, no LPR-cut signal while oil lifts imported-inflation risk (PBOC/新华网/人民网).
chinaChanged: PBOC Jul 15 buyout RR landed: CNY1.4tn (14000亿元) 6-month operation, CNY900bn maturing same day → net +CNY500bn (5000亿元); Jul 3-month and 6-month buyout ops together net +CNY700bn for the month vs net withdrawal last month (PBOC/新华网/央视网).

Q2 GDP 4.3% y/y (NBS, Jul 15) missed ~4.5% consensus — weakest since Q4 2022; Q2 q/q +0.9% in line; H1 GDP +4.7%; fixed-asset investment -5.7% H1; industrial production +5.3% June; retail sales +1.0% June; urban unemployment 5.0% (NBS).

A-shares Wed close weak: Shanghai -0.29% to 3,955.58, CSI 300 -0.20% to 4,786.78, ChiNext -1.21% to 3,804, STAR 50 -4.25%; semiconductors led declines (德明利跌停), pharma/innovative drugs outperformed; ~CNY2.57tn turnover (华尔街见闻 3776980).
H-shares rallied: Hang Seng +1.40% to 24,681.10, Hang Seng Tech +1.30% on CPI relief and tech bid (RTHK/华尔街见闻 3776980) — sharp A/H divergence.

华尔街见闻 (Jul 15 3776948): CPI miss lifted US equities and cooled July hike bets; notes tariff pass-through in core goods and warns core PCE may print hotter than CPI — Fed hold narrative intact; China June exports +27% y/y, H1 +17.6%.
BlockBeats/律动 (Jul 15 flash 302775): market message that Nvidia will resume H20 chip sales in China with US government licensing assurance.
Trade: Trump announced 19% tariff on Indonesian imports (vs threatened 32%), with Indonesia pledging $150bn US energy, $45bn farm goods, 50 Boeing jets (华尔街见闻 3776948/新华社).
chinaImplies: CNY500bn buyout RR net injection cushions Jul 15–17 tax-period funding stress, but does not neutralize oil-driven PPI/import-bill risk — quantity easing ≠ rate pivot.
Nvidia H20 resumption (BlockBeats) is a positive supply-chain signal for China AI hardware, but A-share semis still fell Wed — onshore positioning lags the Korea/US memory rally.
H-shares caught the global CPI bid (+1.40%) while A-shares did not; weak Q2 GDP keeps ChiNext fragile even if Warsh's Senate tone stays non-committal on near-term hikes.
chinaDivergences: Kospi +6.24% and SK Hynix +8.83% domestically vs Shanghai -0.29% but Hang Seng +1.40% — CPI relief hits Korea/H-shares hardest while onshore A-shares lag on GDP disappointment and semis profit-taking. PBOC injecting CNY500bn while A-shares fall = liquidity is ample but risk appetite is sector-selective, not broad beta.

assetFramework.regime: Fed path via real yields — CPI/PPI-vs-oil tug decides the discount rate; AI/semi earnings a secondary support
assetFramework.driver: Post-CPI S&P +0.38% to 7,543.59, Nasdaq +0.90%; July hike odds ~17% post-PPI (CME); ASML FY26 sales raise; IBM -25% on Q2 miss
assetFramework.read: Rates-relief beta held: Nasdaq/semis led while IBM's software miss was idiosyncratic, not a market regime flip
assetFramework.invalidator: Equities fall while yields also fall for 2+ sessions — would signal earnings/growth fear replacing the rates regime

assetFramework.regime: Inflation prints vs consensus + Fed communication — data-led range trading
assetFramework.driver: June CPI and PPI both missed; 10y ~4.57% post-CPI (-~6bp); Warsh Senate Jul 15: no forward guidance, House Q&A 'not mission accomplished'
assetFramework.read: CPI/PPI beta dominated the move; Warsh's hawkish frame capped the rally rather than reversing it
assetFramework.invalidator: 10y back above 4.65% without a hot data print — fiscal-supply or term-premium regime taking over

assetFramework.regime: Rate differentials with a safe-haven bid on Hormuz headlines
assetFramework.driver: July hike odds ~17% post-CPI/PPI; Brent settled $85.90 Jul 15 on Iran blockade
assetFramework.read: Softer rates pull vs oil/geo bid roughly offset — no decisive break; regime intact
assetFramework.invalidator: DXY breaks lower while September hike odds hold ~50% — would suggest flows, not rates, in control

assetFramework.regime: Two-layer: classic real-yield beta day-to-day, central-bank/fiscal-hedge floor structurally
assetFramework.driver: COMEX settled +1.60% at $4,061.10 Jul 14 as 10y fell ~6bp; Brent settled higher Jul 15 at $85.90
assetFramework.read: Traded the real-yield beta on the CPI close; oil settling higher shows the CPI/oil tension, not a floor break
assetFramework.invalidator: Gold falls >3% on a day real yields decline — would question the hedge-demand floor

assetFramework.regime: Geopolitical supply risk — Hormuz blockade premium dominates demand signals
assetFramework.driver: Brent settled $85.90 Jul 15 (+1.38%); Jul 14 settle $84.73; Iran-only blockade reinstated, 20% Hormuz fee scrapped
assetFramework.read: Premium persists despite fee U-turn — supply-risk beta is pricing the barrel, overwriting June CPI/PPI energy relief
assetFramework.invalidator: Brent settles below $78 with Hormuz crossings >40/day — premium unwound

assetFramework.regime: Domestic growth/liquidity vs selective global risk beta — A/H split
assetFramework.driver: Shanghai -0.29%, ChiNext -1.21%, STAR 50 -4.25%; Q2 GDP 4.3% miss; Hang Seng +1.40%; PBOC net +CNY500bn buyout RR
assetFramework.read: Liquidity landed but A-shares ignored it; H-shares caught the global CPI bid — domestic beta lost to growth disappointment + semis profit-taking
assetFramework.invalidator: Shanghai reclaims 4,000 on rising volume with DR007 ≤1.40% — liquidity beta back in charge

assetFramework.regime: PBOC fix as the policy signal; quantity easing without price easing
assetFramework.driver: 7-day rate held 1.40%; CNY1.4tn buyout RR net +CNY500bn; oil lifts imported-inflation risk
assetFramework.read: Stable-fix regime — PBOC eases via quantity while oil argues against an LPR cut
assetFramework.invalidator: Fix persistently weaker than model estimates for a week — would signal tolerated depreciation

assetFramework.regime: High-beta global liquidity asset in the oil→Fed chain — not digital gold in this shock
assetFramework.driver: CPI/PPI cooled July hike odds to ~17%; Brent settled $85.90; BlockBeats Nvidia H20 China headline
assetFramework.read: Still priced as liquidity beta — crypto policy headlines are secondary to the Fed-odds/oil chain
assetFramework.invalidator: BTC rallies while Brent >$85 and September hike odds rise — would signal decoupling from the rates chain

signals.name: Core disinflation pivot — CPI and PPI both miss
signals.evidence: CPI core flat m/m, 2.6% y/y (vs ~2.9% expected); headline -0.4% m/m, 3.5% y/y (vs ~3.8%); PPI headline -0.3% m/m (vs unchanged), 5.5% y/y; core PPI +0.2% m/m (vs +0.3%); July hike odds ~17% post-PPI (BLS; CME FedWatch/CNBC).
signals.mechanism: Back-to-back downside surprises vs consensus ease near-term re-hike urgency and support duration/semis — unless Fed officials dismiss prints as backward-looking.
signals.disprovedIf: September hike probability reclaims >70% and 10y yields break above 4.65% within one week.

signals.name: AI/memory trade rebound despite IBM warning
signals.evidence: SK Hynix +8.83% / ADR +27.29%; ASML FY26 sales raised to €43–45bn (second raise); Kospi +6.24% with buy-side sidecar; IBM -25% on capex shift to hardware (ASML IR; Yonhap; SEC 8-K).
signals.mechanism: CPI relief + equipment demand visibility outweigh legacy IT spend crowding-out; Korea's leveraged semi positioning amplifies the bounce.
signals.disprovedIf: Kospi gives back >50% of Wednesday's gain by Friday and ASML orders miss in Q3.

signals.name: Hormuz blockade premium persists despite fee reversal
signals.evidence: Brent settled $85.90 Jul 15 (+1.38%); Jul 14 settle $84.73; US reinstated Iran-only blockade Jul 14 4pm ET; Trump scrapped 20% Hormuz fee (Investing.com/AP/Bloomberg).
signals.mechanism: Supply-risk and shipping disruption raise forward inflation expectations, limiting how much June CPI/PPI relief can reprice Fed path.
signals.disprovedIf: Brent settles below $78 for three sessions and daily Hormuz crossings exceed 40 without new escalation.

signals.name: Warsh hawkish frame survives CPI/PPI misses
signals.evidence: House Q&A Jul 14: 'not mission accomplished'; Senate hearing Jul 15 with identical prepared remarks — 'no tolerance' for elevated inflation, no forward guidance; September hike ~50-50 post-PPI (Fed/CNN/CNBC).
signals.mechanism: Chair sets interpretive filter — markets must weigh backward-looking CPI/PPI against forward oil shock; keeps September hike pricing alive.
signals.disprovedIf: Warsh explicitly downplays September hike risk in post-hearing press and cites June disinflation as sufficient progress.

signals.name: A-shares lag global semi/CPI relief; H-shares catch bid
signals.evidence: Shanghai -0.29%, STAR 50 -4.25% Wed vs Hang Seng +1.40% and Kospi +6.24%; Q2 GDP 4.3% miss; PBOC net +CNY500bn buyout RR; semis led A-share declines (华尔街见闻 3776980/RTHK).
signals.mechanism: Domestic GDP disappointment and onshore semi profit-taking absorb liquidity injections without broad beta rally — H-shares track global CPI relief, A-shares do not.
signals.disprovedIf: CSI 300 reclaims 4,850 with northbound inflows >CNY5bn for two consecutive sessions.

signals.name: Tariff pass-through lurking beneath soft CPI
signals.evidence: 华尔街见闻 3776948 cites tariff pass-through in core goods and warns core PCE may print hotter than CPI; but June PPI core +0.2% m/m also missed hawkish +0.3% consensus (华尔街见闻; BLS).
signals.mechanism: Headline disinflation may be energy-driven while goods tariffs feed into PCE with a lag — but soft PPI argues upstream pressure is easing, not building.
signals.disprovedIf: Core PCE prints ≥0.3% m/m and Warsh cites goods inflation as a reason to stay hawkish.

watchItems.headline: TSMC revenue/capex guide after Kospi +6%
watchItems.why: STRONG AI/memory rebound signal; tests whether CPI relief sustains the chip rally through Taiwan's bellwether.
watchItems.watch: TSMC Jul 16 revenue/capex guide; Kospi holding 7,200; SOX vs Mon -4.78% close.
watchItems.trigger: TSMC guides below consensus and Kospi closes below 7,000 within 3 sessions.
watchItems.invalidator: TSMC capex guide above street and Kospi holds 7,250+ through Friday.

watchItems.headline: Hormuz blockade vs. Brent settle band
watchItems.why: STRONG energy-premium signal; forward inflation risk that can overwrite June CPI/PPI.
watchItems.watch: Brent daily settle (Jul 15: $85.90); CENTCOM statements; vessel counts through Hormuz.
watchItems.trigger: Brent sustains above $86 on settle for 3 sessions or crossings stay below 20/day.
watchItems.invalidator: Brent settle below $80 with crossings >40/day and no new military strikes for 5 days.

watchItems.headline: September hike odds post double disinflation miss
watchItems.why: STRONG CPI/PPI signal meets MODERATE Warsh hawkish filter; September now ~50-50 per CNBC post-PPI.
watchItems.watch: CME FedWatch September/October hike probability; 10y yield vs ~4.57% post-CPI.
watchItems.trigger: September hike probability reclaims >65% with Brent >$86 settle.
watchItems.invalidator: September hike odds fall below 35% with core PCE ≤0.2% m/m.

watchItems.headline: PBOC buyout RR execution vs. onshore equity response
watchItems.why: MODERATE China liquidity signal; CNY500bn net landed but A-shares still fell on GDP/semis.
watchItems.watch: DR007 vs 1.40% policy rate; follow-on OMO Jul 15–17; Jul 22 LPR fixing.
watchItems.trigger: DR007 rises above 1.50% for two sessions despite Jul 15 net injection.
watchItems.invalidator: DR007 stays ≤1.40% and Shanghai reclaims 4,000 on rising volume.

watchItems.headline: Contrarian check — CPI/PPI disinflation fully priced
watchItems.why: If Kospi +6% and yields -6bp all point dovish, crowded trades are long semis/short dollar.
watchItems.watch: 10y reclaiming 4.65% while Kospi falls >3% in one session.
watchItems.trigger: Both conditions met = markets rejecting disinflation thesis.
watchItems.invalidator: September hike odds fall below 40% with Brent settle below $82.

sources: Bureau of Labor Statistics (June CPI, June PPI), Federal Reserve (Warsh testimony Jul 14–15), CME FedWatch, Reuters, CNBC, AP, Morningstar/Dow Jones, ASML IR, SEC (IBM 8-K, JPM 8-K), Yonhap, Businesskorea, NBS, RTHK, 新华网/人民网/央视网, 华尔街见闻 (3776948/3776980), BlockBeats/律动 (flash 302775), Bloomberg, CENTCOM, Investing.com.
singleSource: Indonesia deal $150bn energy pledge (Trump social/新华社 via 华尔街见闻); IBM worst-day claim vs Oct 1987 (FactSet via Forbes/CNBC).
